description: Create a GitLab CI pipeline using the golang:1.9 Docker image with two stages: test and deploy. Add a coverage job in the test stage that caches vendor/, runs go get github.com/golang/dep/cmd/dep, go get github.com/ory/go-acc, creates a symlink /builds to /go/src/gitlab.com, changes directory to the project, runs dep ensure, executes go-acc ./..., and generates an HTML coverage report with go tool cover -html=coverage.txt -o coverage.html, then stores coverage.html as an artifact. Add a test job also in the test stage that caches .cache/ and vendor/, sets variable CACHE to .cache, runs go get github.com/golang/dep/cmd/dep, go get github.com/alecthomas/gometalinter, go get jrubin.io/zb, creates the same symlink, cd to the project, runs dep ensure, installs gometalinter, runs gometalinter --vendor, and runs zb test. Finally, add a pages job in the deploy stage that depends on coverage, runs only on the master branch, creates a public directory, moves coverage.html to index.html and then into public/, and publishes the public/ directory as a Pages artifact.
